# tokens: # Behavior dispatch spec — covers `compare` and `canon` capabilities
# installed via the unified `behave` word.
#   behave compare/q  (fn [[T T] [Integer] [body]])
#   behave canon/q    (fn [[T]   [String]  [body]])
#   behave nodify/q  (fn [[T]   [Any]     [body]])
# `nodify` projects a value into a Node or Scalar (the AQL data
# subset) rather than into a JSON string — callers compose with a
# separate encoder when they need wire-format JSON. The default
# (when no nodify is registered) returns the value unchanged.
# Three dispatch layers:
#   1. CORE comparators / canonisers — kernel scalar Behaviors
#      (Integer, Decimal, Number cross-promotion, String, Boolean,
#      Atom) hold the canonical `Compare` and `Format` impls.
#   2. NATIVE comparators / canonisers — lang-layer domain types
#      (Date, Instant, ClkDuration, …) attach Compare / Format to
#      their existing FormatBehavior in lang/engine/native_temporal.go.
#   3. USER-DEFINED — `behave compare/q`, `behave canon/q`, and
#      `behave nodify/q` install AQL bodies via
#      lang/engine/native_behave.go's userBehavior wrapper. All three
#      capability slots coexist on one wrapper.
# 1a. CORE compare: kernel scalar Comparers
# Integer ordering — numberCompareBehavior on TNumber handles
# Integer-Integer pairs via the lattice walk plus Decimal pairs.
2 5 lt
5 2 lt
5 5 lt
5 5 lte
5 5 gte
5 2 gt
# Decimal ordering — same dispatch path, AsNumber coerces.
# Cross-type Integer + Decimal: LCA = Number; the Comparer on Number
# handles the entire numeric branch — no per-pair Behavior.
3.0 3 lte
# String lexicographic ordering.
'apple' 'banana' lt
'banana' 'apple' lt
'same' 'same' lt
# Boolean: false < true.
false true lt
true false lt
true true lt
# Atom: lex order by name.
red/q green/q lt
green/q red/q lt
red/q red/q lt
# Cross-branch scalars order by branch precedence
# (Atom < Boolean < Number < String < Path) via the Scalar Comparer.
1 'a' lt
1 true lt
# 1b. CORE canon: kernel default rendering through CanonValue
# Each scalar value renders to its canonical AQL source form (the
# round-trippable representation produced by eng.CanonValue).
'hello'
true
false
red/q
# 2a. NATIVE compare: predefined domain Comparers
# Constructed via the aql:time module — the test runner pre-installs
# its exports so rows can use `time.unix`, `time.seconds`, … directly.
(0 time.unix) (100 time.unix) lt
(100 time.unix) (0 time.unix) lt
(50 time.unix) (50 time.unix) lt
(50 time.unix) (50 time.unix) lte
(0 time.unix) (100 time.unix) gt
(5 time.seconds) (10 time.seconds) lt
(10 time.seconds) (5 time.seconds) lt
# 2b. NATIVE canon: domain Format Behaviors
# Each native type's FormatBehavior produces a domain-specific render
# (Instant in RFC3339Nano, ClkDuration as a Go duration string, …).
0 time.unix
100 time.unix
5 time.seconds
# 3a. USER-DEFINED compare: behave compare/q (fn [[T T] [Integer] [body]])
# `behave compare/q` installs a Comparer on type T. The body receives the
# operands as the defs `a` and `b`; the trailing Integer determines
# ordering: negative → a<b, zero → equal, positive → a>b.
# Ascending by age.
def Person refine Object {age:Integer} behave compare/q (fn [[Person Person] [Integer] [(a 'age' get) (b 'age' get) sub]]) def alice (make Person {age:30}) def bob (make Person {age:25}) alice bob lt
def Person refine Object {age:Integer} behave compare/q (fn [[Person Person] [Integer] [(a 'age' get) (b 'age' get) sub]]) def alice (make Person {age:30}) def bob (make Person {age:25}) bob alice lt
def Person refine Object {age:Integer} behave compare/q (fn [[Person Person] [Integer] [(a 'age' get) (b 'age' get) sub]]) def alice (make Person {age:30}) def bob (make Person {age:25}) alice bob gt
# Descending: swap operands.
def Item refine Object {n:Integer} behave compare/q (fn [[Item Item] [Integer] [(b 'n' get) (a 'n' get) sub]]) def i1 (make Item {n:1}) def i9 (make Item {n:9}) i1 i9 lt
def Item refine Object {n:Integer} behave compare/q (fn [[Item Item] [Integer] [(b 'n' get) (a 'n' get) sub]]) def i1 (make Item {n:1}) def i9 (make Item {n:9}) i9 i1 lt
# Self-equality.
def Person refine Object {age:Integer} behave compare/q (fn [[Person Person] [Integer] [(a 'age' get) (b 'age' get) sub]]) def alice (make Person {age:30}) alice alice lt
def Person refine Object {age:Integer} behave compare/q (fn [[Person Person] [Integer] [(a 'age' get) (b 'age' get) sub]]) def alice (make Person {age:30}) alice alice lte
# Sig validation: heterogeneous input params rejected for compare.
def Person refine Object {age:Integer} behave compare/q (fn [[Person Integer] [Integer] [42]])
expected: ERROR:both params must be the same type

# tokens: # Return type validation.
def Person refine Object {age:Integer} behave compare/q (fn [[Person Person] [String] ['oops']])
# Builtin type override refused.
behave compare/q (fn [[Integer Integer] [Integer] [0]])
# Unknown behavior name.
behave bogus/q (fn [[Integer Integer] [Integer] [0]])
# Body error propagates through the Comparer chain.
def Faulty refine Object {x:Integer} behave compare/q (fn [[Faulty Faulty] [Integer] [quux]]) def v1 (make Faulty {x:1}) def v2 (make Faulty {x:2}) v1 v2 lt
# 3b. USER-DEFINED canon: behave canon/q (fn [[T] [String] [body]])
# `behave canon/q` installs a Format-via-canon Behavior on T. The body
# receives the value as the def `a` and returns a String — that
# String becomes the value's canonical render (via Value.String,
# eng.Canon, every error message).
# Render Person as just its name field. The body returns the raw
# string contents — that becomes the canonical render for Person
# values (Canon → CanonValue's default fallthrough → Value.String →
# userBehavior.Format → body output).
def Person refine Object {name:String age:Integer} behave canon/q (fn [[Person] [String] [(a 'name' get)]]) make Person {name:'Alice' age:30}
# Multi-field canon body — string concat plus a fixed suffix.
def Person refine Object {name:String age:Integer} behave canon/q (fn [[Person] [String] [(a 'name' get) ' (person)' add]]) make Person {name:'Alice' age:30}
# Canon coexists with compare on the same type — both wrap one
# userBehavior; installing the second does not lose the first.

def Person refine Object {name:String age:Integer} behave compare/q (fn [[Person Person] [Integer] [(a 'age' get) (b 'age' get) sub]]) behave canon/q (fn [[Person] [String] [(a 'name' get)]]) def alice (make Person {name:'Alice' age:30}) def bob (make Person {name:'Bob' age:25}) alice bob lt
note: compare still works after canon installed

# tokens: def Person refine Object {name:String age:Integer} behave compare/q (fn [[Person Person] [Integer] [(a 'age' get) (b 'age' get) sub]]) behave canon/q (fn [[Person] [String] [(a 'name' get)]]) make Person {name:'Alice' age:30}
note: canon still works after compare installed

# tokens: # Sig validation: canon must take exactly 1 param.
def Person refine Object {name:String} behave canon/q (fn [[Person Person] [String] ['x']])
# Return-type validation.
def Person refine Object {name:String} behave canon/q (fn [[Person] [Integer] [42]])
# 4. JSONIFY: project a value into a Node or Scalar
# `behave nodify/q (fn [[T] [Any] [body]])` installs a projection on T;
# the body's return value is a Node or Scalar (data-shape, NOT a
# JSON-encoded string). Two access words:
#   - `nodify VALUE` — runs the projection, returns the Node/Scalar
#     directly. Used here for spec assertions on the body output.
#   - `jsonify VALUE` — runs the projection THEN serialises via the
#     JSON encoder. Existing word — the customisation hook now flows
#     through it transparently.
# With no jsonify behavior registered for the type, `nodify` returns
# the value unchanged (Map / List / scalar are already data-shaped).
# 4a. Default pass-through for native data shapes.
5 nodify
'hello' nodify
true nodify
[1 2 3] nodify
{a:1 b:2} nodify
# 4b. NATIVE pass-through — domain types with no jsonify behavior
# attached also pass through unchanged.
0 time.unix nodify
# 4c. USER-DEFINED — a Person with a custom jsonify projects to a
# Map with renamed / filtered fields. The body returns a Map (Node);
# `nodify` exposes that Map directly so the spec can assert on its
# structure without going through the JSON-string encoder.
def Person refine Object {name:String age:Integer secret:String} behave nodify/q (fn [[Person] [Any] [{n: (a 'name' get), age: (a 'age' get)}]]) make Person {name:'Alice' age:30 secret:'hush'} nodify
# Body can return Scalar, not just Node — projection is free to
# collapse the whole instance to a primitive.
def Money refine Object {cents:Integer} behave nodify/q (fn [[Money] [Any] [(a 'cents' get)]]) make Money {cents:1234} nodify
# Compose with compare + canon on the same type — three capability
# slots coexist on one userBehavior wrapper.

def Item refine Object {n:Integer} behave compare/q (fn [[Item Item] [Integer] [(a 'n' get) (b 'n' get) sub]]) behave canon/q (fn [[Item] [String] [(a 'n' get) ' (item)' add]]) behave nodify/q (fn [[Item] [Any] [{value: (a 'n' get)}]]) def i (make Item {n:42}) i nodify
# Sig validation — jsonify takes exactly 1 param.
def Foo refine Object {x:Integer} behave nodify/q (fn [[Foo Foo] [Any] [42]])
# 5. PREDICATE-TYPE DISPATCH
# `def Positive fn [n:Integer Integer [(n gt 0) guard n]]` declares a
# refinement of Integer. InstallType mints Positive's *Type with
# parent=Integer (because PredicateInputType returns the concrete
# input type). `def x:Positive 5` validates via the predicate body
# and then rewraps x with VType=Positive — the carrier idea from
# BEHAVIORS.0.md.
# Result: behaviors installed on Positive dispatch on values
# bound through typed-def; the structural-vs-nominal gotcha is
# resolved for predicate types.
# Without a Positive Comparer installed, x y lt falls through to
# Integer's lattice ancestor (Number's Comparer) — preserves the
# default ordering.
def Positive fn [n:Integer Integer [(n gt 0) guard n]] def x:Positive 5 def y:Positive 10 x y lt
note: default numeric ordering inherited via Positive→Integer→Number

# tokens: # Installing Positive's Comparer (descending: `b a sub`) flips the
# result. This row is the canary: if the typed-def rewrap fails,
# Positive's Comparer is never found and lt stays at `true` (the
# Integer default). It returns `false` only when dispatch finds
# Positive's Comparer — proving the gotcha is closed.
def Positive fn [n:Integer Integer [(n gt 0) guard n]] behave compare/q (fn [[Positive Positive] [Integer] [b a sub]]) def x:Positive 5 def y:Positive 10 x y lt
note: Positive's descending Comparer wins → 5 lt 10 = false

# tokens: # typeof on a typed-bound value reports the asserted type — Positive
# in canonical form is just its leaf name.
def Positive fn [n:Integer Integer [(n gt 0) guard n]] def x:Positive 5 typeof x
# Validation gate still fires — a value failing the predicate body
# (here, -3 doesn't satisfy `n gt 0`) is rejected at bind time.
def Positive fn [n:Integer Integer [(n gt 0) guard n]] def x:Positive -3 x
# A Positive value still satisfies `is Integer` — the lattice walk
# (Positive→Integer) keeps the parent relationship intact, so
# downstream typed positions accepting Integer accept a Positive too.
def Positive fn [n:Integer Integer [(n gt 0) guard n]] def x:Positive 5 x is Integer
# Canon override works on a predicate type — body returns a String
# rendering.
def Positive fn [n:Integer Integer [(n gt 0) guard n]] behave canon/q (fn [[Positive] [String] ['+'  a add]]) def x:Positive 7 x
note: canon body produces a String render

# tokens: # 6. OBJECT-TYPE DISPATCH ON TYPED DEFS
# `def x:Person {map}` now builds a Person-typed ObjectInstance via
# make-style construction. The result carries VType=Person so
# behaviors installed on Person dispatch on x — closes the
# structural-vs-nominal gap for object types alongside the predicate
# fix above.
# Without a Person Comparer, two Persons can't be compared (the LCA
# walk from Person goes to TObjectType which has no Comparer).
def Person refine Object {name:String age:Integer} def x:Person {name:'A' age:30} typeof x
note: def x:Person {map} now produces a Person-tagged instance

# tokens: # Person Comparer dispatches on values bound via typed def.

def Person refine Object {name:String age:Integer} behave compare/q (fn [[Person Person] [Integer] [(a 'age' get) (b 'age' get) sub]]) def x:Person {name:'A' age:30} def y:Person {name:'B' age:25} x y lt
note: 30 lt 25 → false; Comparer wired through typed-def

# tokens: # `def x:Person make Person {map}` accepts a pre-made instance.
def Person refine Object {name:String age:Integer} def x:Person make Person {name:'A' age:30} typeof x
note: pre-made ObjectInstance accepted by typed def

# tokens: # 7. SORT — uses Comparer dispatch
# `LIST sort` returns a new List sorted ascending using
# eng.CompareValues; `MAP sort` returns a new Map ordered by values.
# Pairs the lattice can't compare fall back to string-form ordering
# so sort stays total.
# Sort a list of integers.
# Sort a list of strings (lexical).
['banana' 'apple' 'cherry'] sort
# Already-sorted is a no-op.
[1 2 3] sort
# Sort uses the user-installed Comparer when one is registered.
# The list of Item instances comes back in `n`-ascending order.
def Item refine Object {n:Integer} behave compare/q (fn [[Item Item] [Integer] [(a 'n' get) (b 'n' get) sub]]) [(make Item {n:5}) (make Item {n:1}) (make Item {n:3})] sort size
note: sort returns a list of three items

# tokens: # A nodify body can project Items to bare integers — we sort first
# (using Item's Comparer), then project to make the spec assertion
# easy. The order in the projected list confirms the Comparer fired.

def Item refine Object {n:Integer} behave compare/q (fn [[Item Item] [Integer] [(a 'n' get) (b 'n' get) sub]]) behave nodify/q (fn [[Item] [Any] [(a 'n' get)]]) [(make Item {n:5}) (make Item {n:1}) (make Item {n:3})] sort each [nodify]
note: sort dispatches via Item's Comparer; nodify projects to ints

# tokens: # Descending: same Items, inverted body.

def Item refine Object {n:Integer} behave compare/q (fn [[Item Item] [Integer] [(b 'n' get) (a 'n' get) sub]]) behave nodify/q (fn [[Item] [Any] [(a 'n' get)]]) [(make Item {n:5}) (make Item {n:1}) (make Item {n:3})] sort each [nodify]
# Sort a map by value — entries appear in ascending value order.
{c:3 a:1 b:2} sort
{z:10 y:5 x:1} sort
# 8. REFINE — bare 1-arg subtypes (`refine BaseType`)
# `refine BaseType` with no body builds a bare nominal subtype: a fresh
# type parented directly at BaseType with no added structure. Paired
# with `def` it mints a named subtype that can carry `behave`
# capabilities and act as a dispatch surface — the shared-ancestor
# pattern, where a comparator installed on a common parent orders
# values of its sibling subtypes.
# A 1-arg refine subtype satisfies its base type via the lattice. The
# `;` ends the def statement so refine binds exactly one argument.
def Foo refine List ; Foo is List
note: a 1-arg refine subtype satisfies its base

# tokens: def Foo refine Map ; Foo is Map
# 1-arg refine rejects a non-type argument.
refine 5
note: refine of a concrete value is rejected

# tokens: # behave installs a Comparer on a 1-arg-refined subtype — it is
# OriginUserDef, so the builtin-target guard does not fire.
def Shape refine List behave compare/q (fn [[Shape Shape] [Integer] [0]]) ; Shape is List
note: behave accepts a refined subtype as its target

# tokens: # 9. REFINE — shared-parent comparator dispatch on scalar bases
# A user comparator installed on a common refine-bare parent dispatches
# for both its concrete inhabitants (subtype VALUES via LCA) and its
# sibling subtype LITERALS. The pattern: mint Item from a kernel scalar
# base, mint Foo and Bar from Item, install a Comparer on Item that
# orders by type-name. y (Bar value) lt x (Foo value) flips from the
# kernel magnitude/lex/alphabetic rule to type-name order — Bar < Foo
# by name regardless of payload — confirming the dispatch reaches Item
# on every hop (fn-sig type → behave install → LCA walk → comparator
# body). Each base scalar (Integer, Decimal, String, Boolean, Atom)
# gets one row asserting y lt x = true (the kernel rule would say
# false for Integer/Decimal/Boolean, false for the String/Atom values
# chosen so the magnitude/lex order also opposes the type-name order).
# Integer base: y=2 > x=1 numerically; comparator inverts via Bar<Foo.

def Item refine Integer ; def Foo refine Item ; def Bar refine Item ; def x:Foo 1 ; def y:Bar 2 ; behave compare/q (fn [[a:Item b:Item] [Integer] [if (a is Type) [(a inspect/s 'struct' get) (b inspect/s 'struct' get) cmp] [((typeof a) inspect/s 'struct' get) ((typeof b) inspect/s 'struct' get) cmp]]]) ; y lt x
note: user Comparer on Integer parent inverts magnitude

# tokens: def Item refine Integer ; def Foo refine Item ; def Bar refine Item ; def x:Foo 1 ; def y:Bar 2 ; behave compare/q (fn [[a:Item b:Item] [Integer] [if (a is Type) [(a inspect/s 'struct' get) (b inspect/s 'struct' get) cmp] [((typeof a) inspect/s 'struct' get) ((typeof b) inspect/s 'struct' get) cmp]]]) ; Bar lt Foo
note: Integer parent: dispatches on sibling type literals too

# tokens: def Item refine Integer ; def Foo refine Item ; def Bar refine Item ; def x:Foo 1 ; def y:Bar 2 ; behave compare/q (fn [[a:Item b:Item] [Integer] [if (a is Type) [(a inspect/s 'struct' get) (b inspect/s 'struct' get) cmp] [((typeof a) inspect/s 'struct' get) ((typeof b) inspect/s 'struct' get) cmp]]]) ; y cmp x
note: Integer parent: cmp matches comparator body output

# tokens: # Decimal base: same pattern, decimal payloads.

def Item refine Decimal ; def Foo refine Item ; def Bar refine Item ; def x:Foo 1.5 ; def y:Bar 2.5 ; behave compare/q (fn [[a:Item b:Item] [Integer] [if (a is Type) [(a inspect/s 'struct' get) (b inspect/s 'struct' get) cmp] [((typeof a) inspect/s 'struct' get) ((typeof b) inspect/s 'struct' get) cmp]]]) ; y lt x

def Item refine Decimal ; def Foo refine Item ; def Bar refine Item ; def x:Foo 1.5 ; def y:Bar 2.5 ; behave compare/q (fn [[a:Item b:Item] [Integer] [if (a is Type) [(a inspect/s 'struct' get) (b inspect/s 'struct' get) cmp] [((typeof a) inspect/s 'struct' get) ((typeof b) inspect/s 'struct' get) cmp]]]) ; Bar lt Foo

def Item refine Decimal ; def Foo refine Item ; def Bar refine Item ; def x:Foo 1.5 ; def y:Bar 2.5 ; behave compare/q (fn [[a:Item b:Item] [Integer] [if (a is Type) [(a inspect/s 'struct' get) (b inspect/s 'struct' get) cmp] [((typeof a) inspect/s 'struct' get) ((typeof b) inspect/s 'struct' get) cmp]]]) ; y cmp x
# String base: ProperString is a sibling kernel subtype of String, so
# the typed-def must walk past Item to the kernel String to unify the
# 'a' / 'b' bodies. Locks in the chain-walk in defTypedHandler.

def Item refine String ; def Foo refine Item ; def Bar refine Item ; def x:Foo 'a' ; def y:Bar 'b' ; behave compare/q (fn [[a:Item b:Item] [Integer] [if (a is Type) [(a inspect/s 'struct' get) (b inspect/s 'struct' get) cmp] [((typeof a) inspect/s 'struct' get) ((typeof b) inspect/s 'struct' get) cmp]]]) ; y lt x
note: user Comparer on String parent (sibling-subtype unify)

# tokens: def Item refine String ; def Foo refine Item ; def Bar refine Item ; def x:Foo 'a' ; def y:Bar 'b' ; behave compare/q (fn [[a:Item b:Item] [Integer] [if (a is Type) [(a inspect/s 'struct' get) (b inspect/s 'struct' get) cmp] [((typeof a) inspect/s 'struct' get) ((typeof b) inspect/s 'struct' get) cmp]]]) ; Bar lt Foo

def Item refine String ; def Foo refine Item ; def Bar refine Item ; def x:Foo 'a' ; def y:Bar 'b' ; behave compare/q (fn [[a:Item b:Item] [Integer] [if (a is Type) [(a inspect/s 'struct' get) (b inspect/s 'struct' get) cmp] [((typeof a) inspect/s 'struct' get) ((typeof b) inspect/s 'struct' get) cmp]]]) ; y cmp x
# Boolean base: x=false, y=true. Kernel orders false<true so y lt x is
# normally false; comparator flips to true via Bar<Foo.

def Item refine Boolean ; def Foo refine Item ; def Bar refine Item ; def x:Foo false ; def y:Bar true ; behave compare/q (fn [[a:Item b:Item] [Integer] [if (a is Type) [(a inspect/s 'struct' get) (b inspect/s 'struct' get) cmp] [((typeof a) inspect/s 'struct' get) ((typeof b) inspect/s 'struct' get) cmp]]]) ; y lt x

def Item refine Boolean ; def Foo refine Item ; def Bar refine Item ; def x:Foo false ; def y:Bar true ; behave compare/q (fn [[a:Item b:Item] [Integer] [if (a is Type) [(a inspect/s 'struct' get) (b inspect/s 'struct' get) cmp] [((typeof a) inspect/s 'struct' get) ((typeof b) inspect/s 'struct' get) cmp]]]) ; Bar lt Foo

def Item refine Boolean ; def Foo refine Item ; def Bar refine Item ; def x:Foo false ; def y:Bar true ; behave compare/q (fn [[a:Item b:Item] [Integer] [if (a is Type) [(a inspect/s 'struct' get) (b inspect/s 'struct' get) cmp] [((typeof a) inspect/s 'struct' get) ((typeof b) inspect/s 'struct' get) cmp]]]) ; y cmp x
# Atom base: x=alpha, y=bravo. Atom values inspect to a function-shape
# payload (no 'type' field), so the value branch must route through
# typeof first to recover the subtype name.

def Item refine Atom ; def Foo refine Item ; def Bar refine Item ; def x:Foo alpha/q ; def y:Bar bravo/q ; behave compare/q (fn [[a:Item b:Item] [Integer] [if (a is Type) [(a inspect/s 'struct' get) (b inspect/s 'struct' get) cmp] [((typeof a) inspect/s 'struct' get) ((typeof b) inspect/s 'struct' get) cmp]]]) ; y lt x
note: user Comparer on Atom parent (typeof-then-inspect path)

# tokens: def Item refine Atom ; def Foo refine Item ; def Bar refine Item ; def x:Foo alpha/q ; def y:Bar bravo/q ; behave compare/q (fn [[a:Item b:Item] [Integer] [if (a is Type) [(a inspect/s 'struct' get) (b inspect/s 'struct' get) cmp] [((typeof a) inspect/s 'struct' get) ((typeof b) inspect/s 'struct' get) cmp]]]) ; Bar lt Foo

def Item refine Atom ; def Foo refine Item ; def Bar refine Item ; def x:Foo alpha/q ; def y:Bar bravo/q ; behave compare/q (fn [[a:Item b:Item] [Integer] [if (a is Type) [(a inspect/s 'struct' get) (b inspect/s 'struct' get) cmp] [((typeof a) inspect/s 'struct' get) ((typeof b) inspect/s 'struct' get) cmp]]]) ; y cmp x
